signals.reasoning: Existing ETH long remains a small bounded-risk management hold with live exchange protection already in place, so this is not an urgent cleanup exit. VETO regime still argues against rotating into anything new while the current ETH exposure stays manageable.

BTC is softening after the prior squeeze day, but this is still more consolidation than clean breakdown. Under VETO and position-management priority, there is no reason to rotate away from the protected ETH hold.
SOL is moving with the same broad crypto tape and does not offer a cleaner management alternative than the existing ETH position. Keep it watch-only under the event brake.
TST remains extended and noisy after its squeeze-style move, which makes fresh action low quality under VETO. Better to stand down and manage the small live ETH position.
STBL can still look interesting mechanically, but event risk plus unstable execution quality keep it out of the action set. Not cleaner than the bounded ETH hold.
PEOPLE sits in recovery mode rather than a clean fresh breakout or clean failure. That keeps it watch-only while ETH remains the active managed position.
GRIFFAIN is weak enough to notice but already messy after the drop, not a clean fresh short. Position management first means avoid forcing a second idea.
VVV is still too headline-sensitive and too unstable to trust under VETO. It does not outrank simply managing the current ETH long.
PENGU is a high-beta momentum name that becomes expensive to manage when the board is vetoed. Watch-only, not an override to the existing ETH position.
LDO still has constructive rebound structure, but not enough separation to justify a rotation. VETO keeps it in the hold bucket.
FARTCOIN remains active and green, but this is exactly the kind of noisy squeeze continuation that becomes low quality under a live event brake. No rotation away from ETH.
